summary: SDK `registrationLabel`'s `enablement` case still emits the bare wire label though `Registration::Enablement` now carries a field; the engine reader refuses bare and demands `enablement(<field>)`.

edit.description: `registrationLabel`'s `case "enablement"` (206-207) still returns the bare `"enablement"` string; 30c52e1 made `Registration`'s `enablement` variant field-carrying (`{ via: "enablement", field: string }`, `sdk/src/kind.ts:51`) but left this one arm unchanged, unlike its three siblings in the same switch (`description-trigger`, `paths-match`, `event`, each already `` `${name}(${registration.field})` ``). Change the arm to `` `enablement(${registration.field})` ``.

Regenerate per the file's own header discipline (re-run the memberless builtin emit `tests/builtin_lock_frozen.rs` derives, re-embed the resulting declaration rows verbatim, never hand-edit) once `registrationLabel` is fixed — the `installed-plugin` kind's `registration` row (currently line 90, `registration = ["enablement"]`) becomes `registration = ["enablement(enabled)"]`.

`the_embedded_builtin_lock_carries_the_installed_plugin_kind_and_no_clause` (line 260) pins the stale wire label: `assert_eq!(plugin.registration, vec!["enablement".to_string()]);`. Update to `vec!["enablement(enabled)".to_string()]` — this test currently asserts the bug's own output as correct.
Same stale pin at line 2185 (`assert_eq!(plugin.registration, vec!["enablement".to_string()]);`), same fix.

`the_embedded_builtin_lock_byte_equals_the_sdk_modules_own_memberless_emit` only byte-compares the SDK's derived lock text against the embedded snapshot — writer vs writer, so a one-sided writer/reader respell like this one stays green. Add the seam's missing half after the byte-compare: parse the derived lock text through `drift::parse_declarations`, take the `installed-plugin` `KindFactRow`, and decode it through `kind::CustomKind::from_kind_fact_row` (`src/kind.rs:551`, `pub`), asserting `Ok(_)` and that the decoded registration set contains `Registration::Enablement { field: "enabled".to_string() }` — serializer output proven readable by the real reader, not just self-consistent against its own snapshot.

tests.asserts: the SDK-derived installed-plugin registration row round-trips through the engine's real lock reader (`CustomKind::from_kind_fact_row`) without a `Vocabulary` error, decoding to the field-carrying `Enablement` variant
the embedded builtin lock's installed-plugin kind fact carries the field-carrying `enablement(enabled)` wire label, not the bare form

acceptance: `registrationLabel` emits `enablement(<field>)`; `src/builtin_lock.toml`'s installed-plugin row carries the field-carrying label; both Rust tests that previously pinned the bare form now assert the corrected value; `tests/builtin_lock_frozen.rs` proves the derived lock decodes through the engine's real reader. `cargo test` and `pnpm --dir sdk test` both green; a harness with an installed-plugin member no longer fails `check` on an emit-regenerated lock.
notes: Field report (centercode 0.0.9), observed at 7410b1d, re-verified on disk at HEAD (ec9fdbc): declarations.ts:206 still returns bare "enablement"; src/builtin_lock.toml:90 still embeds it; tests/installed_plugin_kind.rs:260 and tests/lock_declaration_rows.rs:2185 both pin the stale value, so they need updating in the same commit. builtin_lock_frozen.rs only byte-compares writer against writer today — the added round-trip closes exactly that gap. scoped at ec9fdbc.

summary: `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS = 5` cites a memory doc that now reads four hops: the gate constant and its own source disagree, and the doc sentence cannot settle which counting is temper's

gate.reason: A human probe rules the hop semantics — the doc text cannot. Fetched live at 420da04 (code.claude.com/docs/en/memory, retrieved 2026-07-16): 'Imported files can recursively import other files, with a maximum depth of four hops.' Both readings survive that sentence: the root's own import is hop 1 and the chain caps at four edges (⇒ 4), or 'imported files ... recursively import' names a recursion four deep ATOP that first import (⇒ 5, today's constant). Only measuring the runtime's actual load depth decides it, the headless-testbed act that produced the `(mention-gate-containment)` evidence — never plan's to guess and never build's to fetch, because guessing here edits a live gate's semantics: a cap too low forges a dead-member finding on a member that loads, a cap too high suppresses a real one (intent.md invariant 2).

edit.description: The constant and the cite that justifies it. `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS = 5` (75) sits under a doc (71-74) asserting 'the `at-import` grammar is recursion-capped at five hops (code.claude.com/docs/en/memory, retrieved 2026-07-02)' — a number the live page does not say. temper's own indexing IS decidable on disk and needs no ruling: `live_members` (650) seeds every own-live member and runs `for _ in 0..MAX_IMPORT_HOPS` BFS rounds (677), one import edge per round, so the constant counts EDGES traversed from a live seed. What no reading of the disk settles is which edges the doc's 'four hops' counts — hence the park. Whatever the ruling: the cite is re-retrieved, re-dated, and re-worded to state the hop-counting it encodes, never left asserting a bare number its source does not carry. The two narration cites deferring to it — 548 ('hop-capped at [`MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`]') and 647-648 ('capped at [`MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`] hops (the `at-import` recursion depth the format documents)') — carry the same claim and move with it, as does the propagation comment at 672-675 ('capped at the format's import recursion depth'). f6731fb (DIRECTIVE_FIELD consolidated into graph.rs ahead of this region, in the d1af9a5..HEAD window) shifted every address below its insertion point; re-read and confirmed at HEAD (0522eff), so every address above now carries at its new line.

`the_import_closure_is_hop_capped` (1547) already pins the boundary mechanically rather than in a comment — `r1..r5` rescued from a live seed, `r6` dead as the sole finding — so it encodes today's 5 in the one place a flip cannot silently miss, and it is the test that moves if the ruling is four. Its comment (1548-1552) spells 'capped at the format's five-hop import recursion' (1550) and carries the same claim as the constant's cite; re-word it to the ruled reading rather than leaving the fixture narrating the retired number. Addresses re-read and confirmed at HEAD (0522eff) after upstream graph.rs/test-file shifts; they carry at these lines.

tests.asserts: the import closure rescues a chain exactly as deep as the ruled cap and no deeper — the boundary hop pinned at the ruled number, in the ruled counting

acceptance: `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`, its doc cite, and the two narration cites that defer to it all agree with the live docs, and each states which hop-counting it encodes (spec-system.md, 'Form rules': an external fact carries its source and retrieval date where it is enforced, verified against current docs, never encoded from memory). The boundary hop stays pinned by a test at the ruled number. `cargo test` green.
notes: Park re-tested at 0522eff and holds — nothing ruled the hop semantics. f6731fb shifted graph.rs's const region from 59/55-58 to 75/71-74 (DIRECTIVE_FIELD moved in ahead of it); value and cite date unchanged (5, 2026-07-02). `files[]` line cites rewritten to match. The park's subject is untouched, and may still be wrong. A build-side friction capture records the same disagreement; the human's to read, not plan's to drain. rescoped 0522eff.

summary: The packaging remainder after the linux-x64+win32-x64 first cut: darwin binaries (Apple notarizing), the plugin/bundle + marketplace.json, and the binary↔npm version lockstep at the v0.1 tag

gate.reason: Human release actions: John's Apple notarizing for the darwin standalone binary + darwin optionalDependency package; the v0.1 launch tag that flips the binary's self-reported crate version (0.1.0) into lockstep with npm's 0.0.x. Channel 3 (plugin/bundle + marketplace.json) is a git repo + marketplace.json + free community web form (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs) — no signing creds — but rides the same release-workflow build-out. release.yml (channel 2, linux-x64+win32-x64) + the SDK launcher/optionalDependencies + standalone tag assets shipped 07-11; the standalone-asset step is wired but unexercised until a tag fires.

edit.description: Extend the existing release.yml (linux-x64 + win32-x64 first cut, the darwin deferral stated at lines 7-9) with the darwin matrix targets (aarch64/x86_64-apple-darwin) + an Apple notarization step, and a channel-3 step producing/publishing the `temper bundle` output with its marketplace.json. Distinct from the standalone tag-asset step, already wired (unexercised until a tag).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper on all three OSes without a Rust toolchain (darwin binary notarized + pinned), or via the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); the binary self-reports 0.1.0 in lockstep with the v0.1 npm cut (distribution.md: prebuilt binaries install on all three OSes; lockstep engine pin).
notes: Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish. Park re-tested on disk at 0522eff, holding on every clause: no v0.1 tag (only era tags), crate 0.1.0, release.yml still states the darwin + channel-3 deferral verbatim. `git diff 68fae5c..HEAD -- .github/` is empty. scoped b676656.
